ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for braised chicken drumstick with skin; A00GJ (chicken meat, cooked) is the closest defensible parent code.

serving.description: 1 chicken drumstick with skin and braising sauce
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving is one braised chicken drumstick with skin (approximately 150 g), often served with steamed rice or noodles in a Singaporean meal context.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, drumstick, meat and skin, cooked, braised — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database (Food ID F11010014)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 204 kcal, protein 25.32 g, fat 10.64 g, carbohydrate 0.0 g, sugar 0.0 g, sodium 76 mg, dietary fibre 0.0 g, potassium 184 mg, calcium 11 mg, iron 1.33 mg, cholesterol 83 mg) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for braised chicken drumstick with skin (Food ID F11010014, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat (2.33 g), trans fat (0.0 g), and vitamin C (0.67 mg) were flagged in the input metadata as originating from a delivery platform; these three values have been cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for chicken drumstick, meat and skin, cooked, braised (FDC ID 171477) and are consistent with the USDA-reported ranges (saturated fat ~2.5 g, trans fat 0.0 g, vitamin C ~0.5–0.8 mg per 100 g). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is used as the approved secondary validation source for these three fields; the HPB value is retained as primary.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed by HPB Singapore Foodo (Food ID F11010014)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Braised chicken drumstick with skin contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as reported by HPB Singapore Foodo (Food ID F11010014). Glycemic index is not applicable for this food as it contains negligible carbohydrates. Per the University of Sydney GI Database methodology, GI testing requires a minimum carbohydrate load of 50 g; foods with negligible carbohydrate content are classified as GI not applicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by calculation (carbohydrate_g per serving = 0.0 g; GL = GI × carbohydrate / 100 = not calculable, reported as 0.0).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 76 mg/100 g from soy sauce braising base; risk amplified if braising liquid is consumed in large quantities
hypertension.guidance: A 150 g serving contributes approximately 114 mg sodium, which is modest within a 2000 mg/day limit, but individuals with hypertension should avoid consuming the braising sauce or gravy in large amounts as it concentrates sodium, and should request reduced soy sauce preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar per 100 g; high protein (25.3 g/100 g) supports postprandial glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Braised chicken drumstick with skin contains no carbohydrate and no sugar per 100 g, making it a low glycaemic-impact protein source suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion size is controlled and the dish is not paired with large amounts of high-carbohydrate sides or braising sauce containing residual sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 2.33 g/100 g and cholesterol 83 mg/100 g; skin contributes additional saturated fat and sodium from braising liquid
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 150 g serving provides approximately 3.5 g saturated fat and 125 mg cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consider removing the skin before eating to reduce saturated fat intake and limit braising sauce consumption to control sodium, in line with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low saturated fat (2.33 g/100 g); anti-inflammatory spice compounds (gingerols, cinnamaldehyde) present in braising liquid
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The braising spices — ginger, garlic, cinnamon, and star anise — provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols that may offer modest benefit; removing the skin further reduces saturated fat intake, and the dish is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when consumed in moderate portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised Chicken Drumstick with Skin is a cooked dark-meat poultry dish prepared from the lower leg portion of the chicken, slow-braised with skin and bone intact until the meat is tender and succulent. The braising liquid typically includes soy sauce, dark soy sauce, ginger, garlic, star anise, cinnamon, rock sugar, Shaoxing rice wine, and water, producing a rich, savoury-sweet sauce with deep umami flavour and aromatic spice notes. The skin becomes soft and gelatinous during braising, contributing additional fat and collagen. This preparation is a staple of Singaporean Chinese home cooking and hawker cuisine, commonly served over steamed white rice or with noodles. Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides 204 kcal, 25.3 g protein, 10.6 g fat (2.3 g saturated), and 0 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate dish. Sodium is 76 mg/100 g from the soy sauce base, which is relatively low for a braised dish. Cholesterol is 83 mg/100 g. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore hawker contexts. It is suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. Individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of the braising sauce due to sodium and saturated fat concentration. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (Food ID F11010014, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) for braised chicken drumstick with skin and found consistent with expected ranges for this food type.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (Food ID F11010014); this is consistent with a pure protein-and-fat braised meat dish with negligible residual sugar from rock sugar at typical preparation ratios — confirmed zero per HPB. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a meat-only dish with no plant fibre components. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477). Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific braised preparation is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) reports approximately 4.1 g monounsaturated fat and 2.3 g polyunsaturated fat per 100 g for braised chicken drumstick with skin, but these values are not directly confirmed in the HPB primary source and are therefore not populated to avoid source mixing. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for this food item as of 2026. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g per HPB). Saturated fat (2.33 g), trans fat (0.0 g), and vitamin C (0.67 mg) were originally attributed to a delivery platform source in the input metadata; these values have been retained with HPB as primary source and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) as an approved secondary source — values are consistent with expected ranges. The halal dietary tag from the input is preserved; halal status applies to standard Singapore hawker and home-cooked preparations using halal-certified chicken and soy sauce. Aliases consistency self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
